standard_description: Understand subtraction of rational numbers as adding the additive inverse, p – q = p + (–q). Show that the distance between two rational numbers on the number line is the absolute value of their difference, and apply this principle in real-world contexts.
subject: Math

problems.text:
Problem 1
Circle the expressions below that have a value of 2.
$${8+ (-6)}$$
$${6-8}$$
$${-{8-6}}$$
$${-8+(-6)}$$
$${8-6}$$
$${-6+8}$$
$${-8+6}$$
$${-6 - (-8)}$$
Problem 2
Rewrite each subtraction problem as an addition problem and each addition problem as a subtraction problem.
a.
$${-8-7}$$
b.
$${11+(-3)}$$
c.
$${20-(-2)}$$
d.
$${-4 + 16}$$

Problem 1
Four points are shown on the number line below.
###IMAGE0###
a.   The value of the expression
$${-2{1\over3}+p}$$
is positive. What point could represent the value of
$$p$$
?
b.   The value of the expression
$$-2{1\over3}-p$$
is positive. What point could represent the value of
$$p$$
?
Problem 2
The expression
$${{3\over4} - {7\over8} }$$
represents the location of point B. Point A is located in a different place on the number line and is the same distance from 0 as point B. Mark the approximate location of point A on the number line below.
###IMAGE1###
Problem 3
Brad’s checking account starts off with a balance of $126.50. Throughout the week, Brad pays a bill for $87.21, and pays $5.64 for breakfast. He deposits $15.00 but then pays another bill for $55.25. At the end of the week, he gets a notice from the bank that he has a negative balance. How much does Brad need to deposit into his account to get to a $0 balance?

Task
Ojos del Salado is the highest mountain in Chile, with a peak at about 6900 meters above sea level. The Atacama Trench, just off the coast of Peru and Chile, is about 8100 meters below sea level (at its lowest point).
What is the difference in elevations between Mount Ojos del Salado and the Atacama Trench?
Is the elevation halfway between the peak of Mount Ojos del Salado and the Atacama Trench above sea level or below sea level? Explain without calculating the exact value.
What elevation is halfway between the peak of Mount Ojos del Salado and the Atacama Trench?

Problem 1
Use your number line and game piece to model each subtraction problem and answer the questions that follow.
$$5-3$$
$$3-5$$
$$-3-5$$
$$-5-3$$
a.   What does each part of the subtraction problem tell you to do on the number line?
b.   What answer do you get for each subtraction problem?
c.   Rewrite each subtraction problem as an addition problem of the opposite value. Use your number line and game piece to model the addition problem and show it has the same value. What changes and what stays the same?
Problem 2
Use your number line and game piece to model each subtraction problem and answer the questions that follow.
$${5-(-3)}$$
$${3-(-5)}$$
$$-{3-(-5)}$$
$$-{5-(-3)}$$
a.   What does each part of the subtraction problem tell you to do on the number line?
b.   What answer do you get for each subtraction problem?
c.   Rewrite each subtraction problem as an addition problem of the opposite value. Use your number line and game piece to model the addition problem and show it has the same value. What changes and what stays the same?
Problem 3
On a number line, you start at
$${{-4}}$$
.
a.   Explain how you can get to
$${-9}$$
. Write an addition problem and a subtraction problem to represent this.
b.   Explain how you can get to 1. Write an addition problem and a subtraction problem to represent this.

Problem 1
Mount Ojos del Salado is the highest mountain in Chile, with a peak at about 6,900 meters above sea level. The Atacama Trench, just off the coast of Peru and Chile, is about 8,100 meters below sea level (at its lowest point).
a.   What is the difference in elevations between Mount Ojos del Salado and the Atacama Trench?
b.   Is the elevation halfway between the peak of Mount Ojos del Salado and the Atacama Trench above sea level or below sea level? Explain without calculating the exact value.
c.   What elevation is halfway between the peak of Mount Ojos del Salado and the Atacama Trench?
Problem 2
Find each sum or difference. Rewrite subtraction problems into addition problems or use a number line as needed.
a.
$${18{1\over5}-(-5)}$$
b.
$${-40{2\over3}-8{1\over2}}$$
c.
$${-9.08+16.52}$$
d.
$${-6-(-12)}$$
e.
$${-11{1\over6}-(-3)}$$
f.
$${52+(-15)}$$
g.
$${-36.125+(-14.6)}$$

Problem 1
A man on a boat is sitting 9 feet above sea level. A bird flies at an altitude of 54 feet above sea level, and a fish
###IMAGE0###
swims below at an altitude of 8 feet below sea level. The ocean floor sits 75 feet below the surface of the ocean.
Find each distance described below. Then write a number sentence to represent each distance you find.
a.   Between the bird and the man
b.   Between the man and the surface of the ocean
c.   Between the man and the fish
d.   Between the ocean floor and the fish
e.   Between the ocean floor and the bird
Problem 2
Aakash, Bao Ying, Chris, and Donna all live on the same street as their school, which runs from east to west.
Aakash lives
$${5{1\over2}}$$
blocks to the west of the school.
Bao Ying lives
$${4{1\over4}}$$
blocks to the east of the school.
Chris lives
$${2{3\over4}}$$
blocks to the west of the school.
Donna lives
$${6{1\over2}}$$
blocks to the east of the school.
a.   Draw a picture that represents the positions of their houses along the street.
b.   How far is each house from every other house?
c.   Represent the relative position of the houses on a number line, with the school at zero, points to the west represented by negative numbers, and points to the east represented by positive numbers.
d.   How can you see the answers to part B on the number line? Using the numbers (some of which are positive and some negative) that label the positions of houses on the number line, represent these distances using sums or differences.
Problem 3
The distance between point Q and point R is 5.3. Give possible values for Q and R in each situation, and represent the distance on a number line.​​​​​​
a.   Q and R are both positive numbers.
###IMAGE1###
b.   Either Q or R is negative.
###IMAGE2###
c.   Both Q and R are negative.
###IMAGE3###

The highest temperature on a winter day in New York City was
$$11$$
°C. The lowest temperature on the same day in the same city was
$$-8$$
°C.
Jordan says that over the course of the day, the temperature decreased by
$$3$$
°C. Do you agree with Jordan? Justify your response.

khan_exercises.title: Absolute value to find distance
khan_exercises.description: Represent the distance between two numbers or measurements as the absolute value of their difference. Distinguish between distance and amount of change in context.

items.question_content:
**Which $2$ of the following expressions represent the distance between $-2$ and $1$?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The distance between two points on the number line is equal to the absolute value of their difference.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Here are the two correct distance expressions:

- $|1 - ({-2})|$

- $| {-2} - 1|$

Note that subtracting a number is the same as adding its opposite.

These expressions represent the distance between $-2$ and $1$.

- $|1+2|$
- $\left\lvert -2 - 1\right\rvert$

items.question_content:
Yair scooped a layer of sawdust into a composting toilet. The sawdust went through the seat and landed at the bottom of the pit $1.5\,\text{m}$ below the ground. The toilet seat is $0.4\,\text{m}$ above the ground.

Answer $2$ questions about the toilet.

1. **What is the distance between the toilet seat and the bottom of the pit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What was the change in height of the sawdust from the toilet seat to the bottom of the pit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in height. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in height first, then the distance.

###Change in height

The seat is above the ground, so it has a height of $0.4\,\text{m}$. The bottom of the pit is below the ground, so it has a height of $-1.5\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in height}&=\text{height}_{\text{ending}}-\text{height}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=\text{height}_{\text{bottom}}-\text{height}_{\text{seat}}\\\\
&=-1.5-(0.4)\\\\
&=-1.9
\end{align}$

The sawdust has a change in height of $-1.9\,\text{m}$. A negative change in height makes sense, because the sand went down.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in height}|\\\\
&=|-1.9|\\\\
&=1.9
\end{align}$

The sawdust went down $1.9\,\text{m}$, so it moved a distance of $1.9\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

The distance between the toilet seat and the bottom of the pit is $1.9\,\text{m}$.

The change in height of the sawdust from the toilet seat to the bottom of the pit is $-1.9\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
A group of people are in a [[☃ definition 1]] for a ceremony. The temperature inside the sweat lodge is $40\degree\text{C}$. Outside the sweat lodge, the temperature is $15\degree\text{C}$.

Answer $2$ questions about the sweat lodge.

1. **How far apart were the temperatures inside and outside of the sweat lodge?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\degree\text{C}$ apart

2. **What was the change in surrounding temperature for a person who went from inside the sweat lodge to outside?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\degree\text{C}$

[[☃ image 3]]
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in temperature. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in temperature first, then the distance.

###Change in temperature

The temperature inside the sweat lodge is $40\degree\text{C}$. Outside the sweat lodge, the temperature is $15\degree\text{C}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in temperature}&=\text{temperature}_{\text{ending}}-\text{temperature}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=\text{temperature}_{\text{outside}}-\text{temperature}_{\text{inside}}\\\\
&=15-40\\\\
&=-25
\end{align}$

The person has a change in temperature of $-25\degree\text{C}$. A negative change in temperature makes sense, because the person went from a warmer place to a cooler place.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance apart}&=|\text{change in temperature}|\\\\
&=|-25|\\\\
&=25
\end{align}$

The temperature was $25\degree\text{C}$ lower outside the sweat lodge, so the temperatures were $25\degree\text{C}$ apart.

###Conclusion

The temperatures inside and outside of the sweat lodge were $25\degree\text{C}$ apart.

The change in surrounding temperature for a person who went from inside the sweat lodge to outside was $-25\degree\text{C}$.

items.question_content:
Jamika is on a diving board that is $5\,\text{m}$ above the water at her swimming lessons. She dives into the water and reaches a depth of $3\,\text{m}$ below the surface.

Answer $2$ questions about Jamika.

1. **What was Jamika's change in height from the diving board to her deepest point?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What is the distance between the diving board and Jamika's deepest point?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in height. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in height first, then the distance.

###Change in height

The diving board is above the water, so it has a height of $5\,\text{m}$. Jamika dives below the water, so she reaches a height of $-3\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in height}&=\text{height}_{\text{ending}}-\text{height}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=-3-(5)\\\\
&=-8
\end{align}$

Jamika has a change in height of $-8\,\text{m}$. A negative change in height makes sense, because Jamika moved downward.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in height}|\\\\
&=|-8|\\\\
&=8
\end{align}$

Jamika went down $8\,\text{m}$, so she moved a distance of $8\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

Jamika's change in height from the diving board to her deepest point was $-8\,\text{m}$.

The distance between the diving board and Jamika's deepest point was $8\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
In a Pueblo folktale, Coyote and Rabbit are having a race. Coyote has run $50\,\text{m}$ from the starting line and Rabbit has run $30\,\text{m}$ from the starting line. Both ran in the same direction.

Answer $2$ questions about the race.

1. **What is the distance between Coyote and Rabbit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What is the change in position, relative to the starting line, for Coyote if he decides to go back to where Rabbit is right now to play a trick on him?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in position. So moving farther from the starting line will have a positive value, and moving closer to the starting line will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in position first, then the distance.

###Change in position

Coyote is past the starting line, so Coyote has a position of $50\,\text{m}$ relative to the starting line. Rabbit is also past the starting line, so he has a position of $30\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in position}&=\text{position}_{\text{ending}}-\text{position}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=\text{position}_{\text{Rabbit}}-\text{position}_{\text{Coyote}}\\\\
&=30-50\\\\
&=-20
\end{align}$

Coyote has a change in position of $-20\,\text{m}$. A negative change in position makes sense, because Coyote moved back closer to the starting line.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in position}|\\\\
&=|-20|\\\\
&=20
\end{align}$

Coyote goes back $20\,\text{m}$, so Coyote moves a distance of $20\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

The distance between Coyote and Rabbit was $20\,\text{m}$.

Coyote's change in position from where he was to where Rabbit was is $-20\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
Samantha points a telescope at an altitude of $32^\circ$ to see the star Pollux. Then she lowers the telescope to an altitude of $25^\circ$ to see a [[☃ definition 1]].

Answer $2$ questions about the telescope.

1. **What was the change in telescope angle from Pollux to the planetary nebula?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$

2. **How many degrees apart were Pollux and the planetary nebula?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $^\circ$

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in angle. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in angle first, then the distance.

###Change in angle

The star Pollux is above the horizon, so it has an angle of $32^\circ$. The planetary nebula is also above the horizon, so it has an angle of $25^\circ$

$\begin{align}
\text{change in angle}&=\text{angle}_{\text{ending}}-\text{angle}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=32-(25)\\\\
&=-7
\end{align}$

The telescope has a change in angle of $-7^\circ$. A negative change in angle makes sense, because Samantha lowered the telescope.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in angle}|\\\\
&=|-7|\\\\
&=7
\end{align}$

The telescope lowered $7^\circ$, so it moved through a span of $7^\circ$.

###Conclusion

The change in telescope angle from Pollux to the planetary nebula was $-7^\circ$.

Pollux and the planetary nebula were $7^\circ$ apart.

items.question_content:
Noa helped his family prepare a holiday meal in their apartment kitchen, $29.3\,\text{m}$ above ground level. Then he took a portion down to his neighbor, who lives $22\,\text{m}$ above ground level in the same building.

Answer $2$ questions about Noa.

1. **What was Noa's change in height from his apartment to the neighbor's apartment?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What is the vertical distance between Noa's apartment and the neighbor's apartment?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in height. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in height first, then the distance.

###Change in height

Noa's apartment is above ground level, so it has a height of $29.3\,\text{m}$. The neighbor's apartment is also above ground level, so it has a height of $22\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in height}&=\text{height}_{\text{ending}}-\text{height}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=22-29.3\\\\
&=-7.3
\end{align}$

Noa has a change in height of $-7.3\,\text{m}$. A negative change in height makes sense, because Noa moved downward.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in height}|\\\\
&=|-7.3|\\\\
&=7.3
\end{align}$

Noa went down $7.3\,\text{m}$, so he moved a vertical distance of $7.3\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

Noa's change in height from his apartment to the neighbor's apartment was $-7.3\,\text{m}$.

The vertical distance between Noa's apartment and the neighbor's apartment was $7.3\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
Eva sees a dolphin $3.2$ meters *below* sea level and a bird $ \dfrac{47}{10}$ meters *above* sea level.

**Which $2$ of the following expressions represents the vertical distance between the dolphin and the bird?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The distance between two points on the number line is equal to the absolute value of their difference.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Let's use a number line model to illustrate this situation. The units are in meters.

[[☃ image 1]]

Either of the following expressions can represent the vertical distance between the dolphin and the bird:

- $\left\lvert\maroonE{\dfrac{47}{10}} - \blueE{\left(-3.2\right)}\right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert\blueE{-3.2} - \maroonE{\dfrac{47}{10}}\right\rvert$

Note that subtracting a number is the same as adding its opposite.

These expressions represent the distance between the dolphin and the bird:

- $\left\lvert\dfrac{47}{10} +3.2\right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert -3.2 - \dfrac{47}{10} \right\rvert$

items.question_content:
**Which $2$ of the following expressions represent the distance between $-2$ and $1$?**

[[☃ image 1]]

items.question_content:
Yair scooped a layer of sawdust into a composting toilet. The sawdust went through the seat and landed at the bottom of the pit $1.5\,\text{m}$ below the ground. The toilet seat is $0.4\,\text{m}$ above the ground.

Answer $2$ questions about the toilet.

1. **What is the distance between the toilet seat and the bottom of the pit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What was the change in height of the sawdust from the toilet seat to the bottom of the pit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in height. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in height first, then the distance.

###Change in height

The seat is above the ground, so it has a height of $0.4\,\text{m}$. The bottom of the pit is below the ground, so it has a height of $-1.5\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in height}&=\text{height}_{\text{ending}}-\text{height}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=\text{height}_{\text{bottom}}-\text{height}_{\text{seat}}\\\\
&=-1.5-(0.4)\\\\
&=-1.9
\end{align}$

The sawdust has a change in height of $-1.9\,\text{m}$. A negative change in height makes sense, because the sand went down.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in height}|\\\\
&=|-1.9|\\\\
&=1.9
\end{align}$

The sawdust went down $1.9\,\text{m}$, so it moved a distance of $1.9\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

The distance between the toilet seat and the bottom of the pit is $1.9\,\text{m}$.

The change in height of the sawdust from the toilet seat to the bottom of the pit is $-1.9\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
A group of people are in a [[☃ definition 1]] for a ceremony. The temperature inside the sweat lodge is $40\degree\text{C}$. Outside the sweat lodge, the temperature is $15\degree\text{C}$.

Answer $2$ questions about the sweat lodge.

1. **How far apart were the temperatures inside and outside of the sweat lodge?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\degree\text{C}$ apart

2. **What was the change in surrounding temperature for a person who went from inside the sweat lodge to outside?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\degree\text{C}$

[[☃ image 3]]
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in temperature. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in temperature first, then the distance.

###Change in temperature

The temperature inside the sweat lodge is $40\degree\text{C}$. Outside the sweat lodge, the temperature is $15\degree\text{C}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in temperature}&=\text{temperature}_{\text{ending}}-\text{temperature}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=\text{temperature}_{\text{outside}}-\text{temperature}_{\text{inside}}\\\\
&=15-40\\\\
&=-25
\end{align}$

The person has a change in temperature of $-25\degree\text{C}$. A negative change in temperature makes sense, because the person went from a warmer place to a cooler place.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance apart}&=|\text{change in temperature}|\\\\
&=|-25|\\\\
&=25
\end{align}$

The temperature was $25\degree\text{C}$ lower outside the sweat lodge, so the temperatures were $25\degree\text{C}$ apart.

###Conclusion

The temperatures inside and outside of the sweat lodge were $25\degree\text{C}$ apart.

The change in surrounding temperature for a person who went from inside the sweat lodge to outside was $-25\degree\text{C}$.

items.question_content:
Jamika is on a diving board that is $5\,\text{m}$ above the water at her swimming lessons. She dives into the water and reaches a depth of $3\,\text{m}$ below the surface.

Answer $2$ questions about Jamika.

1. **What was Jamika's change in height from the diving board to her deepest point?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What is the distance between the diving board and Jamika's deepest point?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in height. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in height first, then the distance.

###Change in height

The diving board is above the water, so it has a height of $5\,\text{m}$. Jamika dives below the water, so she reaches a height of $-3\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in height}&=\text{height}_{\text{ending}}-\text{height}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=-3-(5)\\\\
&=-8
\end{align}$

Jamika has a change in height of $-8\,\text{m}$. A negative change in height makes sense, because Jamika moved downward.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in height}|\\\\
&=|-8|\\\\
&=8
\end{align}$

Jamika went down $8\,\text{m}$, so she moved a distance of $8\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

Jamika's change in height from the diving board to her deepest point was $-8\,\text{m}$.

The distance between the diving board and Jamika's deepest point was $8\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
In a Pueblo folktale, Coyote and Rabbit are having a race. Coyote has run $50\,\text{m}$ from the starting line and Rabbit has run $30\,\text{m}$ from the starting line. Both ran in the same direction.

Answer $2$ questions about the race.

1. **What is the distance between Coyote and Rabbit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What is the change in position, relative to the starting line, for Coyote if he decides to go back to where Rabbit is right now to play a trick on him?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in position. So moving farther from the starting line will have a positive value, and moving closer to the starting line will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in position first, then the distance.

###Change in position

Coyote is past the starting line, so Coyote has a position of $50\,\text{m}$ relative to the starting line. Rabbit is also past the starting line, so he has a position of $30\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in position}&=\text{position}_{\text{ending}}-\text{position}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=\text{position}_{\text{Rabbit}}-\text{position}_{\text{Coyote}}\\\\
&=30-50\\\\
&=-20
\end{align}$

Coyote has a change in position of $-20\,\text{m}$. A negative change in position makes sense, because Coyote moved back closer to the starting line.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in position}|\\\\
&=|-20|\\\\
&=20
\end{align}$

Coyote goes back $20\,\text{m}$, so Coyote moves a distance of $20\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

The distance between Coyote and Rabbit was $20\,\text{m}$.

Coyote's change in position from where he was to where Rabbit was is $-20\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
Samantha points a telescope at an altitude of $32^\circ$ to see the star Pollux. Then she lowers the telescope to an altitude of $25^\circ$ to see a [[☃ definition 1]].

Answer $2$ questions about the telescope.

1. **What was the change in telescope angle from Pollux to the planetary nebula?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$

2. **How many degrees apart were Pollux and the planetary nebula?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $^\circ$

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in angle. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in angle first, then the distance.

###Change in angle

The star Pollux is above the horizon, so it has an angle of $32^\circ$. The planetary nebula is also above the horizon, so it has an angle of $25^\circ$

$\begin{align}
\text{change in angle}&=\text{angle}_{\text{ending}}-\text{angle}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=32-(25)\\\\
&=-7
\end{align}$

The telescope has a change in angle of $-7^\circ$. A negative change in angle makes sense, because Samantha lowered the telescope.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in angle}|\\\\
&=|-7|\\\\
&=7
\end{align}$

The telescope lowered $7^\circ$, so it moved through a span of $7^\circ$.

###Conclusion

The change in telescope angle from Pollux to the planetary nebula was $-7^\circ$.

Pollux and the planetary nebula were $7^\circ$ apart.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in height. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in height first, then the distance.

###Change in height

Noa's apartment is above ground level, so it has a height of $29.3\,\text{m}$. The neighbor's apartment is also above ground level, so it has a height of $22\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in height}&=\text{height}_{\text{ending}}-\text{height}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=22-29.3\\\\
&=-7.3
\end{align}$

Noa has a change in height of $-7.3\,\text{m}$. A negative change in height makes sense, because Noa moved downward.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

&=|-7.3|\\\\
&=7.3

Noa went down $7.3\,\text{m}$, so he moved a vertical distance of $7.3\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

The vertical distance between Noa's apartment and the neighbor's apartment was $7.3\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
Eva sees a dolphin $3.2$ meters *below* sea level and a bird $ \dfrac{47}{10}$ meters *above* sea level.

**Which $2$ of the following expressions represents the vertical distance between the dolphin and the bird?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The distance between two points on the number line is equal to the absolute value of their difference.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Let's use a number line model to illustrate this situation. The units are in meters.

[[☃ image 1]]

Either of the following expressions can represent the vertical distance between the dolphin and the bird:

- $\left\lvert\maroonE{\dfrac{47}{10}} - \blueE{\left(-3.2\right)}\right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert\blueE{-3.2} - \maroonE{\dfrac{47}{10}}\right\rvert$

Note that subtracting a number is the same as adding its opposite.

These expressions represent the distance between the dolphin and the bird:

- $\left\lvert\dfrac{47}{10} +3.2\right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert -3.2 - \dfrac{47}{10} \right\rvert$

items.question_content:
A baby mango tree stands $2.6$ meters *above* the ground. Its roots extend $ \dfrac7{5}$ meters *below* the ground. 

**Which $2$ of the following expressions represents the vertical distance between the top of the mango tree and the tips of its roots?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The distance between two points on the number line is equal to the absolute value of their difference.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Let's use a number line model to illustrate this situation. The units are in meters.

[[☃ image 1]]

Either of the following expressions can represent the vertical distance between the tree top and roots:

- $\left\lvert\maroonE{2.6} - \blueE{\left(-\dfrac75\right)}\right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert\blueE{-\dfrac75} - \maroonE{2.6}\right\rvert$

Note that subtracting a number is the same as adding its opposite.

These expressions represent the vertical distance between the top of the mango tree and the tips of its roots:

- $\left\lvert{2.6} +\dfrac{7}{5}\right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert{-\dfrac{7}{5}} - {2.6}\right\rvert$

items.question_content:
An ant is on top of a tree $23$ meters tall, directly *above* her home, which is $0.3$ meters *below* the ground. 

**Which $2$ of the following expressions represents the distance between the ant and her home?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The distance between two points on the number line is equal to the absolute value of their difference.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Let's use a number line model to illustrate this situation. The units are in meters.

[[☃ image 1]]

Either of the following expressions can represent the distance between the ant and her home:

- $|\maroonE{23} - \blueE{(-0.3)}|$

- $|\blueE{-0.3} - \maroonE{23}|$

Note that subtracting a number is the same as adding its opposite.

These expressions represent the distance between the ant and her home:

- $|23 + 0.3|$

- $|-0.3 - 23|$

items.question_content:
Mona is hang gliding $ \dfrac{5}{4}$ kilometers *above* sea level. Directly below her, she sees a whale $0.2$ kilometers *below* sea level.

**Which $2$ of the following expressions represents the distance between Mona and the whale?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The distance between two points on the number line is equal to the absolute value of their difference.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Let's use a number line model to illustrate this situation. The units are in kilometers.

[[☃ image 1]]

Either of the following expressions can represent the distance between Mona and the whale:

- $\left\lvert\maroonE{\dfrac54} - \blueE{\left(-0.2\right)}\right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert\blueE{-0.2} - \maroonE{\dfrac54}\right\rvert$

Note that subtracting a number is the same as adding its opposite.

These expressions represent the vertical distance between Mona and the whale:

- $\left\lvert \dfrac{5}{4} + 0.2 \right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert -0.2 - \dfrac{5}{4} \right\rvert$

items.question_content:
Natasha and her two dogs were walking on a perfectly straight road when her two dogs ran away from her in opposite directions. Her beagle is now $\dfrac{25}{4}$ meters directly to her *right*, and her labrador is $\dfrac{51}{20}$ meters directly to her *left*.

khan_exercises.description: Solve conceptually challenging absolute value problems. In order to succeed at these problems, you'll need to remember that the absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those numbers.

Absolute value to find distance challenge

items.question_content:
**Order the following expressions from least to greatest.**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0b7d4c7dfe300014f1ace80240f8ca089702da06)

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. For example, $\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ is the distance between $a$ and $b$. Remember that distance (and absolute value!) is never negative.
Since $a$ is negative and the other expressions use absolute value (i.e. distance), $a$ is the expression with the least value.

$\left\lvert b - c \right\rvert < \left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ because the distance between $b$ and $c$ is less than the distance between $a$ and $b$. 

$\left\lvert a - b \right\rvert < \left\lvert a - c \right\rvert$  because the distance between $a$ and $b$ is less than the distance between $a$ and $c$.

The expressions are ordered from least to greatest below:

 | | ||
- | - | - |- 
$a$ | $\left\lvert b - c\right\rvert$ | $\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$| $\left\lvert a - c\right\rvert$

items.question_content:
**Order the following expressions from least to greatest.**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/325cc1693a963299158f285acc6e82403b1cd409)

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. For example, $\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ is the distance between $a$ and $b$. Remember that distance (and absolute value!) is never negative.
Since $b$ is negative and the other expressions use absolute value (i.e. distance), $b$ is the expression with the least value.

$\left\lvert a - c \right\rvert < \left\lvert b - c\right\rvert$ because the distance between $a$ and $c$ is less than the distance between $b$ and $c$. 

$\left\lvert b - c \right\rvert < \left\lvert a - b \right\rvert$  because the distance between $b$ and $c$ is less than the distance between $a$ and $b$.

The expressions are ordered from least to greatest below:

 | | ||
- | - | - |- 
$b$ | $\left\lvert a - c\right\rvert$ | $\left\lvert b - c\right\rvert$| $\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$

items.question_content:
**Order the following expressions from least to greatest.**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/790011bd4e40e22709ae9267c0ef062238b0ed5b)

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. For example, $\left\lvert x - y\right\rvert$ is the distance between $x$ and $y$. Remember that distance (and absolute value!) is never negative.
Since $z$ is negative and the other expressions use absolute value (i.e. distance), $z$ is the expression with the least value.

$\left\lvert y - z \right\rvert < \left\lvert x - y\right\rvert$ because the distance between $y$ and $z$ is less than the distance between $x$ and $y$. 

$\left\lvert x - y \right\rvert < \left\lvert x - z \right\rvert$  because the distance between $x$ and $y$ is less than the distance between $x$ and $z$.

The expressions are ordered from least to greatest below:

 | | ||
- | - | - |- 
$z$ | $\left\lvert y - z\right\rvert$ | $\left\lvert x - y \right\rvert$| $\left\lvert x - z\right\rvert$

items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions are equivalent to $\left\lvert a -b\right\rvert$?**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/31a3f027f421a4fc4509907c03d587f4ab82c9e2)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. Remember that distance (and absolute value!) is never negative.

$\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ is the distance between $a$ and $b$.
$a-b$ is negative because we are subtracting a positive number $(b)$ from a negative number $(a)$.
$-(b-a)$ is negative because we are taking the opposite of  subtracting a negative number $(a)$ from a positive number $(b)$.
The correct answer is "None of the above."

items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions are equivalent to $\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$?**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0bf2540b422c75093070ca1bcc2d5846a7c91079)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. Remember that distance (and absolute value!) is never negative.

$\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ is the distance between $a$ and $b$.
$\left\lvert a+b\right\rvert \neq \left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ because $\left\lvert a+b\right\rvert$ is greater than the distance between $a$ and $b$.
$a-b \neq \left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ because $a-b$ is negative.
The correct answer is "None of the above."

items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions are equivalent to $\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$?**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0bf2540b422c75093070ca1bcc2d5846a7c91079)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. Remember that distance (and absolute value!) is never negative.

$\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ is the distance between $a$ and $b$.
$\left\lvert a\right\rvert -\left\lvert b\right\rvert = \left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ because $\left\lvert a\right\rvert >\left\lvert b\right\rvert$ and both numbers are on the same side of $0$.

$\left\lvert a\right\rvert +\left\lvert b\right\rvert \neq \left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ because $\left\lvert a\right\rvert + \left\lvert b\right\rvert$ is the total distance that $a$ and $b$ are from $0$. This is *not* equal to the distance between $a$ and $b$ because they are the same side of $0$.

The following expression is equivalent to $\left\lvert a -b\right\rvert$:

- $\left\lvert a\right\rvert -\left\lvert b\right\rvert$

items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions are *greater than* $\left\lvert h - g\right\rvert$?**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d0d0ea863ee8388a31a042b9d2e61a210b3607a0)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. Remember that distance (and absolute value!) is never negative.

For example, $\left\lvert h - g\right\rvert$ is the distance between $h$ and $g$.
$\left\lvert j - h\right\rvert> \left\lvert h - g\right\rvert$ because the distance between $j$ and $h$ is greater than the distance between $h$ and $g$.
$-k > \left\lvert h - g\right\rvert$ because $-k$ is equal to the distance between $0$ and $k$, which is greater than the distance between $h$ and $g$.

The following expressions are greater than $\left\lvert h -g\right\rvert$:

- $\left\lvert j - h\right\rvert$

- $-k$

items.question_content:
**If $y > z$, which of the following equations must be true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $y > z$ tells us that $y \neq z$.

The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. For example, $\left\lvert y - z\right\rvert$ is the distance between $y$ and $z$. Remember that distance (and absolute value!) is never negative.

$\left\lvert y - z\right\rvert$ and  $\left\lvert z - y\right\rvert$ both represent the distance between $y$ and $z$ so $\left\lvert y - z\right\rvert$ and  $\left\lvert z - y\right\rvert$ must both be positive.

The following equations must be true:

- $\left\lvert y - z\right\rvert > 0$

- $\left\lvert z - y\right\rvert > 0$

items.question_content:
**Which of the $x$ values are solutions to the following inequality?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7cd0c171a862e408b6919bf8b33a81b129fc4ceb)

$\qquad \left\lvert x - a \right\rvert > a$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. For example, $\left\lvert x - a\right\rvert$ is the distance between $x$ and $a$.

If $x = b$, then $\left\lvert x - a\right\rvert$ is the distance between $b$ and $a$.

The distance between $b$ and $a$ is **greater than** $a$.

If $x = c$, then $\left\lvert x - a\right\rvert$ is the distance between $c$ and $a$.

The distance between $c$ and $a$ is  **less than** $a$.

The following $x$ values are solutions to $\left\lvert x - a \right\rvert > a$ :

- $x = b$

items.question_content:
**Select the best interpretation of the following inequality.**

$\qquad \left\lvert -2.2 - x\right\rvert > 3$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. For example, $\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ is the distance between $a$ and $b$.
$\left\lvert -2.2 - x\right\rvert > 3$ means that the distance between $-2.2$ and $x$ is greater than $3$.

choices.content: The distance between $-2.2$ and $-x$ is greater than $3$.
The distance between $-2.2$ and $x$ is greater than $3$.
The distance between $3$ and $-2.2$ is greater than $x$.

items.question_content:
**Select the best interpretation of the following equation.**

$\qquad \left\lvert11 - x\right\rvert = \left\lvert y - 3\right\rvert$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line.

$\left\lvert 11 - x \right\rvert$ is the distance between $11$ and $x$.

 $\left\lvert y - 3 \right\rvert$ is the distance between $y$ and $3$.
$\left\lvert11 - x\right\rvert = \left\lvert y - 3\right\rvert$ means that the distance between $11$ and $x$ is equal to the distance between $y$ and $3$.

choices.content: The distance between $11$ and $x$ is equal to the distance between $y$ and $3$.
The distance between $11$ and $-x$ is equal to the distance between $y$ and $-3$.
The distance between $11$ and $y$ is equal to the distance between $-x$ and $-3$.

items.question_content:
**Select the best interpretation of the following equation.**

$\qquad \left\lvert11 - y\right\rvert = 3$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The absolute value of the difference of two numbers is the distance between those two numbers on the number line. For example, $\left\lvert a - b\right\rvert$ is the distance between $a$ and $b$.
$\left\lvert11 - y\right\rvert = 3$ means that the distance between $11$ and $y$ is $3$.

items.question_content:
**If $w$ and $q$ are numbers, which of the following expressions will *always* be the distance between $w$ and $q$ on the number line?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The expression that will always be the distance between $w$ and $q$ on the number line must use absolute value because distance cannot be negative.
$\left\lvert w + q\right\rvert$ cannot be the distance between $w$ and $q$ because, for example, this expression suggests that the distance between $w = -3$ and $q = 3$ is $0$. (It's $6$!)
$\left\lvert w - q\right\rvert$ will *always* be the distance between $w$ and $q$ on the number line.

items.question_content:
**Match each expression with its appropriate interpretation.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: Subtracting $9$ from $7$ is the number $9$ to the left of $7$ on the horizontal number line.
The absolute value of the difference of two numbers $(7$ and $9)$ is the distance between those two numbers on the number line.
A number $(7)$ and its opposite $(-7)$ always sum to zero.

The expressions and interpretations are matched below:

Expression | Interpretation
:-: | :-:
$7 - 9$ | The number $9$ to the left of $7$ on the horizontal number line
$\left\lvert 7 - 9\right\rvert$ | The distance between $7$ and $9$ on the number line
$7 + (-7)$ | Sums to zero

right: The number $9$ to the left of $7$ on the horizontal number line
The distance between $7$ and $9$ on the number line

items.question_content:
Yair scooped a layer of sawdust into a composting toilet. The sawdust went through the seat and landed at the bottom of the pit $1.5\,\text{m}$ below the ground. The toilet seat is $0.4\,\text{m}$ above the ground.

Answer $2$ questions about the toilet.

1. **What is the distance between the toilet seat and the bottom of the pit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What was the change in height of the sawdust from the toilet seat to the bottom of the pit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in height. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in height first, then the distance.

###Change in height

The seat is above the ground, so it has a height of $0.4\,\text{m}$. The bottom of the pit is below the ground, so it has a height of $-1.5\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in height}&=\text{height}_{\text{ending}}-\text{height}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=\text{height}_{\text{bottom}}-\text{height}_{\text{seat}}\\\\
&=-1.5-(0.4)\\\\
&=-1.9
\end{align}$

The sawdust has a change in height of $-1.9\,\text{m}$. A negative change in height makes sense, because the sand went down.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in height}|\\\\
&=|-1.9|\\\\
&=1.9
\end{align}$

The sawdust went down $1.9\,\text{m}$, so it moved a distance of $1.9\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

The distance between the toilet seat and the bottom of the pit is $1.9\,\text{m}$.

The change in height of the sawdust from the toilet seat to the bottom of the pit is $-1.9\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
A group of people are in a [[☃ definition 1]] for a ceremony. The temperature inside the sweat lodge is $40\degree\text{C}$. Outside the sweat lodge, the temperature is $15\degree\text{C}$.

Answer $2$ questions about the sweat lodge.

1. **How far apart were the temperatures inside and outside of the sweat lodge?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\degree\text{C}$ apart

2. **What was the change in surrounding temperature for a person who went from inside the sweat lodge to outside?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\degree\text{C}$

[[☃ image 3]]
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in temperature. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in temperature first, then the distance.

###Change in temperature

The temperature inside the sweat lodge is $40\degree\text{C}$. Outside the sweat lodge, the temperature is $15\degree\text{C}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in temperature}&=\text{temperature}_{\text{ending}}-\text{temperature}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=\text{temperature}_{\text{outside}}-\text{temperature}_{\text{inside}}\\\\
&=15-40\\\\
&=-25
\end{align}$

The person has a change in temperature of $-25\degree\text{C}$. A negative change in temperature makes sense, because the person went from a warmer place to a cooler place.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance apart}&=|\text{change in temperature}|\\\\
&=|-25|\\\\
&=25
\end{align}$

The temperature was $25\degree\text{C}$ lower outside the sweat lodge, so the temperatures were $25\degree\text{C}$ apart.

###Conclusion

The temperatures inside and outside of the sweat lodge were $25\degree\text{C}$ apart.

The change in surrounding temperature for a person who went from inside the sweat lodge to outside was $-25\degree\text{C}$.

items.question_content:
Jamika is on a diving board that is $5\,\text{m}$ above the water at her swimming lessons. She dives into the water and reaches a depth of $3\,\text{m}$ below the surface.

Answer $2$ questions about Jamika.

1. **What was Jamika's change in height from the diving board to her deepest point?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What is the distance between the diving board and Jamika's deepest point?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in height. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in height first, then the distance.

###Change in height

The diving board is above the water, so it has a height of $5\,\text{m}$. Jamika dives below the water, so she reaches a height of $-3\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in height}&=\text{height}_{\text{ending}}-\text{height}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=-3-(5)\\\\
&=-8
\end{align}$

Jamika has a change in height of $-8\,\text{m}$. A negative change in height makes sense, because Jamika moved downward.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in height}|\\\\
&=|-8|\\\\
&=8
\end{align}$

Jamika went down $8\,\text{m}$, so she moved a distance of $8\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

Jamika's change in height from the diving board to her deepest point was $-8\,\text{m}$.

The distance between the diving board and Jamika's deepest point was $8\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
In a Pueblo folktale, Coyote and Rabbit are having a race. Coyote has run $50\,\text{m}$ from the starting line and Rabbit has run $30\,\text{m}$ from the starting line. Both ran in the same direction.

Answer $2$ questions about the race.

1. **What is the distance between Coyote and Rabbit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What is the change in position, relative to the starting line, for Coyote if he decides to go back to where Rabbit is right now to play a trick on him?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in position. So moving farther from the starting line will have a positive value, and moving closer to the starting line will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in position first, then the distance.

###Change in position

Coyote is past the starting line, so Coyote has a position of $50\,\text{m}$ relative to the starting line. Rabbit is also past the starting line, so he has a position of $30\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in position}&=\text{position}_{\text{ending}}-\text{position}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=\text{position}_{\text{Rabbit}}-\text{position}_{\text{Coyote}}\\\\
&=30-50\\\\
&=-20
\end{align}$

Coyote has a change in position of $-20\,\text{m}$. A negative change in position makes sense, because Coyote moved back closer to the starting line.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in position}|\\\\
&=|-20|\\\\
&=20
\end{align}$

Coyote goes back $20\,\text{m}$, so Coyote moves a distance of $20\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

The distance between Coyote and Rabbit was $20\,\text{m}$.

Coyote's change in position from where he was to where Rabbit was is $-20\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
Samantha points a telescope at an altitude of $32^\circ$ to see the star Pollux. Then she lowers the telescope to an altitude of $25^\circ$ to see a [[☃ definition 1]].

Answer $2$ questions about the telescope.

1. **What was the change in telescope angle from Pollux to the planetary nebula?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$

2. **How many degrees apart were Pollux and the planetary nebula?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $^\circ$

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in angle. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in angle first, then the distance.

###Change in angle

The star Pollux is above the horizon, so it has an angle of $32^\circ$. The planetary nebula is also above the horizon, so it has an angle of $25^\circ$

$\begin{align}
\text{change in angle}&=\text{angle}_{\text{ending}}-\text{angle}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=32-(25)\\\\
&=-7
\end{align}$

The telescope has a change in angle of $-7^\circ$. A negative change in angle makes sense, because Samantha lowered the telescope.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in angle}|\\\\
&=|-7|\\\\
&=7
\end{align}$

The telescope lowered $7^\circ$, so it moved through a span of $7^\circ$.

###Conclusion

The change in telescope angle from Pollux to the planetary nebula was $-7^\circ$.

Pollux and the planetary nebula were $7^\circ$ apart.

items.question_content:
Noa helped his family prepare a holiday meal in their apartment kitchen, $29.3\,\text{m}$ above ground level. Then he took a portion down to his neighbor, who lives $22\,\text{m}$ above ground level in the same building.

Answer $2$ questions about Noa.

1. **What was Noa's change in height from his apartment to the neighbor's apartment?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$

2. **What is the vertical distance between Noa's apartment and the neighbor's apartment?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Strategy

The **distance** between two numbers is equal to the absolute value of their difference. So distance is always non-negative.

On the other hand, **change** has a direction. We are measuring change in height. So an increase will have a positive value, and a decrease will have a negative value.

Since we start with the difference for both, let's find the change in height first, then the distance.

###Change in height

Noa's apartment is above ground level, so it has a height of $29.3\,\text{m}$. The neighbor's apartment is also above ground level, so it has a height of $22\,\text{m}$.

$\begin{align}
\text{change in height}&=\text{height}_{\text{ending}}-\text{height}_{\text{starting}}\\\\
&=22-29.3\\\\
&=-7.3
\end{align}$

Noa has a change in height of $-7.3\,\text{m}$. A negative change in height makes sense, because Noa moved downward.

###Distance

Now we can take the absolute value.

$\begin{align}
\text{distance}&=|\text{change in height}|\\\\
&=|-7.3|\\\\
&=7.3
\end{align}$

Noa went down $7.3\,\text{m}$, so he moved a vertical distance of $7.3\,\text{m}$.

###Conclusion

Noa's change in height from his apartment to the neighbor's apartment was $-7.3\,\text{m}$.

The vertical distance between Noa's apartment and the neighbor's apartment was $7.3\,\text{m}$.

items.question_content:
Eva sees a dolphin $3.2$ meters *below* sea level and a bird $ \dfrac{47}{10}$ meters *above* sea level.

**Which $2$ of the following expressions represents the vertical distance between the dolphin and the bird?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The distance between two points on the number line is equal to the absolute value of their difference.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Let's use a number line model to illustrate this situation. The units are in meters.

[[☃ image 1]]

Either of the following expressions can represent the vertical distance between the dolphin and the bird:

- $\left\lvert\maroonE{\dfrac{47}{10}} - \blueE{\left(-3.2\right)}\right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert\blueE{-3.2} - \maroonE{\dfrac{47}{10}}\right\rvert$

Note that subtracting a number is the same as adding its opposite.

These expressions represent the distance between the dolphin and the bird:

- $\left\lvert\dfrac{47}{10} +3.2\right\rvert$

- $\left\lvert -3.2 - \dfrac{47}{10} \right\rvert$
